title: Getting started with hypnoR
text: hypnoR hypnogram layer Circadia Lab R ecosystem. takes staged hypnogram anywhere – mrpheus (full AASM: W / N1 / N2 / N3 / REM), zeitR (coarse actigraphy-derived: W / Sleep / Quiet sleep), hand-built tibble – turns sleep architecture metrics, NREM/REM cycles, transition statistics, publication-ready plots. Every metric plotting function staging-agnostic: computes whatever possible given resolution actually present, returns NA (documented per-function) metrics need finer staging ’s available. article walks whole pipeline using real recording: mrpheus’s bundled SC4001E0 example – 22-hour Sleep-EDF cassette recording, already staged mrpheus’s automatic sleep-staging model.

title: Getting started with hypnoR
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("circadia-bio/hypnoR")

title: Getting started with hypnoR
text: staging mrpheus’s raw per-epoch output: one row per 30-second epoch, AASM stage label, five posterior probabilities underlying LightGBM classifier. mrpheus::export_hypnogram() prepares hypnoR: (real recording start time comes EDF header mrpheus::read_edf() time; bundled .rds doesn’t carry , use placeholder based note mrpheus’s sleep-staging-demo article cassette started around 16:13 local time.) new_hypnogram() – constructor every hypnoR function goes – accepts directly: Staging resolution (aasm , since N1 / N2 / N3 / REM present) epoch duration auto-detected stored attributes hyp – every hypnoR function reads rather asking repeat .
code: staging <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "SC4001E0_staging.rds", package = "mrpheus")) staging |>    head() #> # A tibble: 6 × 7 #>   epoch stage prob_N1 prob_N2 prob_N3 prob_REM prob_W #>   <int> <chr>   <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>    <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1     1 W     0.111   0.00803 0.00192  0.0335   0.846 #> 2     2 W     0.00878 0.0121  0.00364  0.00233  0.973 #> 3     3 W     0.00261 0.00409 0.00178  0.00269  0.989 #> 4     4 W     0.0621  0.0575  0.00901  0.0220   0.849 #> 5     5 W     0.102   0.143   0.0260   0.0845   0.644 #> 6     6 W     0.00921 0.0110  0.0137   0.00935  0.957 mrp_hyp <- mrpheus::export_hypnogram(   staging,   epoch_s        = 30,   start_time     = as.POSIXct("2024-01-01 16:13:00", tz = "UTC"),   participant_id = "SC4001" ) #> ✔ Hypnogram ready: 2650 epochs. Pass to `hypnor::new_hypnogram()` once hypnor is available. hyp <- new_hypnogram(mrp_hyp) hyp #>  #> ── hypnoR hypnogram ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ℹ Epochs: 2650 #> ℹ Epoch length: 30 s #> ℹ Resolution: aasm #> ℹ Subject: SC4001 #> # A tibble: 2,650 × 5 #>    epoch time                stage subject_id source  #>  * <int> <dttm>              <ord> <chr>      <chr>   #>  1     1 2024-01-01 16:13:00 W     SC4001     mrpheus #>  2     2 2024-01-01 16:13:30 W     SC4001     mrpheus #>  3     3 2024-01-01 16:14:00 W     SC4001     mrpheus #>  4     4 2024-01-01 16:14:30 W     SC4001     mrpheus #>  5     5 2024-01-01 16:15:00 W     SC4001     mrpheus #>  6     6 2024-01-01 16:15:30 W     SC4001     mrpheus #>  7     7 2024-01-01 16:16:00 W     SC4001     mrpheus #>  8     8 2024-01-01 16:16:30 W     SC4001     mrpheus #>  9     9 2024-01-01 16:17:00 W     SC4001     mrpheus #> 10    10 2024-01-01 16:17:30 W     SC4001     mrpheus #> # ℹ 2,640 more rows

title: Getting started with hypnoR
text: Two optional cleanup steps happen metric sees hypnogram.

title: Getting started with hypnoR
text: Automatic per-epoch staging temporal continuity constraint – – can produce brief, isolated stage flips don’t reflect real sleep physiology (single REM epoch nested inside N2 run, instance). smooth_hypnogram() offers two label-rules: aasm_isolated reassigns single epoch flanked identically sides; min_run merges run shorter min_run_epochs whichever flanking run longer, regardless whether flanks agree. original, unsmoothed labels always preserved stage_raw comparison.
code: hyp_smooth <- smooth_hypnogram(hyp, method = c("aasm_isolated", "min_run"), min_run_epochs = 4) mean(hyp_smooth$stage != hyp_smooth$stage_raw) #> [1] 0.1283019

title: Getting started with hypnoR
text: SC4001 22-hour ambulatory recording – ordinary daytime Wake one real sleep period. Running metrics entire recording badly distort things like sleep onset latency NREM/REM cycle counts, window_hypnogram() restricts hypnogram time epoch window metric sees : compute_sleep_architecture() also accepts lights_off/lights_on directly windows internally, ’d rather pass real lights-/timestamps derive window data .
code: sleep_idx    <- which(as.character(hyp_smooth$stage) != "W") onset_epoch  <- hyp_smooth$epoch[sleep_idx[1]] offset_epoch <- hyp_smooth$epoch[sleep_idx[length(sleep_idx)]]  hyp_sleep <- window_hypnogram(hyp_smooth, from_epoch = onset_epoch, to_epoch = offset_epoch) nrow(hyp_sleep) #> [1] 1858

title: Getting started with hypnoR
code: arch <- compute_sleep_architecture(hyp_sleep) arch #> # A tibble: 1 × 14 #>   tst_min tib_min se_pct sol_min waso_min rem_lat_min sws_lat_min pct_n1 pct_n2 #>     <dbl>   <dbl>  <dbl>   <dbl>    <dbl>       <dbl>       <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1    372.     929   40.0       0     558.           0         361   6.46   41.2 #> # ℹ 5 more variables: pct_n3 <dbl>, pct_rem <dbl>, pct_sleep <dbl>, #> #   pct_quiet_sleep <dbl>, staging_resolution <chr>

title: Getting started with hypnoR
code: trans <- compute_transitions(hyp_sleep) trans$matrix #> # A tibble: 5 × 6 #>   from       N3     N2      N1     REM      W #>   <chr>   <dbl>  <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 N3    0.949   0.0233 0       0.00465 0.0233 #> 2 N2    0.0196  0.928  0.00980 0.00980 0.0327 #> 3 N1    0       0.0625 0.792   0.0208  0.125  #> 4 REM   0.00578 0.0116 0.0116  0.879   0.0925 #> 5 W     0.00359 0.0108 0.00448 0.0143  0.967 trans$fragmentation #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   n_transitions fragmentation_index wake_transitions #>           <int>               <dbl>            <int> #> 1           101              0.0544               37

title: Getting started with hypnoR
Getting started with hypnoR
text: Two hypnogram styles available. "step" (default) classic clinical trace:  "capsule" draws rounded-pill bars per contiguous stage run, one lane per stage:
code: plot_hypnogram(hyp_sleep, cycles = cyc) plot_hypnogram(hyp_sleep, style = "capsule") plot_architecture(arch) plot_transition_matrix(trans$matrix)

title: Getting started with hypnoR
text: Everything works identically coarse, actigraphy-derived staging – ’s whole point “staging-agnostic.” zeitR ships bundled ActTrust validation recording; running full rest-activity pipeline exporting produces coarse (W / Sleep / Quiet sleep) hypnogram: Resolution auto-detected coarse (N1/N2/N3/REM labels present), every metric function adapts automatically – separate “coarse mode” argument anywhere: Notice rem_lat_min, sws_lat_min, AASM stage percentages (pct_n1/pct_n2/pct_n3/pct_rem) NA – need finer staging three states can provide – pct_sleep/pct_quiet_sleep populated instead. compute_transitions() works exactly way, just 3x3 matrix instead 5x5: compute_cycles() one function ’s genuinely AASM-– NREM/REM cycle segmentation needs REM stage segment , errors clearly rather silently returning nonsense: Plotting works identically , 3-lane layout instead 5:
code: result <- zeitR::run_pipeline(   system.file("extdata", "input1.txt", package = "zeitR"),   tz    = "UTC",   quiet = TRUE )  zeitr_hyp <- zeitR::export_hypnogram(result) hyp_coarse <- new_hypnogram(zeitr_hyp) hyp_coarse #> # A tibble: 76,196 × 5 #>    epoch time                stage subject_id source #>  * <int> <dttm>              <ord> <chr>      <chr>  #>  1     1 2021-05-27 11:10:15 W     input1     zeitR  #>  2     2 2021-05-27 11:11:15 W     input1     zeitR  #>  3     3 2021-05-27 11:12:15 W     input1     zeitR  #>  4     4 2021-05-27 11:13:15 W     input1     zeitR  #>  5     5 2021-05-27 11:14:15 W     input1     zeitR  #>  6     6 2021-05-27 11:15:15 W     input1     zeitR  #>  7     7 2021-05-27 11:16:15 W     input1     zeitR  #>  8     8 2021-05-27 11:17:15 W     input1     zeitR  #>  9     9 2021-05-27 11:18:15 W     input1     zeitR  #> 10    10 2021-05-27 11:19:15 W     input1     zeitR  #> # ℹ 76,186 more rows arch_coarse <- compute_sleep_architecture(hyp_coarse) arch_coarse #> # A tibble: 1 × 14 #>   tst_min tib_min se_pct sol_min waso_min rem_lat_min sws_lat_min pct_n1 pct_n2 #>     <dbl>   <dbl>  <dbl>   <dbl>    <dbl>       <dbl>       <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1  12078.   38098   31.7     376    25554          NA          NA     NA     NA #> # ℹ 5 more variables: pct_n3 <dbl>, pct_rem <dbl>, pct_sleep <dbl>, #> #   pct_quiet_sleep <dbl>, staging_resolution <chr> trans_coarse <- compute_transitions(hyp_coarse) trans_coarse$matrix #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>   from        `Quiet sleep`   Sleep       W #>   <chr>               <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1 Quiet sleep       0.926   0.0655  0.00814 #> 2 Sleep             0.630   0.219   0.151   #> 3 W                 0.00500 0.00469 0.990 compute_cycles(hyp_coarse) #> Error in `compute_cycles()`: #> ! `compute_cycles()` requires a full AASM hypnogram. #> ✖ `hypnogram` has "coarse" resolution (no REM stage), so NREM/REM cycles cannot #>   be detected. plot_hypnogram(hyp_coarse) plot_architecture(arch_coarse)

title: Getting started with hypnoR
text: read_hypnogram() – ingesting hypnograms directly CSV, EDF annotations, YASA, Compumedics, Nox export files – still development. now, hypnograms come upstream package (mrpheus::export_hypnogram(), zeitR::export_hypnogram()) hand-built tibble passed new_hypnogram(), shown . See vignette("mrpheus-integration", package = "hypnoR") vignette("zeitR-integration", package = "hypnoR") (“Worked examples” section pkgdown site) deeper, warts--walks cleaning interpreting real staging output source.

title: Cleaning up and interpreting real staging: a worked example with mrpheus
code: staging <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "SC4001E0_staging.rds", package = "mrpheus"))  mrp_hyp <- mrpheus::export_hypnogram(   staging,   epoch_s        = 30,   start_time     = as.POSIXct("2024-01-01 16:13:00", tz = "UTC"),   participant_id = "SC4001" ) #> ✔ Hypnogram ready: 2650 epochs. Pass to `hypnor::new_hypnogram()` once hypnor is available.  hyp <- new_hypnogram(mrp_hyp)

what: First look: a lot of REM, in odd places
title: Cleaning up and interpreting real staging: a worked example with mrpheus
text: Two things jump . First, REM appears scattered across nearly whole 22-hour recording, including well plain daytime Wake. Second, trace looks lot busier smooth textbook hypnograms ’re used seeing.
code: plot_hypnogram(hyp)

title: Cleaning up and interpreting real staging: a worked example with mrpheus
text: Worth checking rather assuming either way. mrpheus::stage_epochs()’s entire staging decision : pure per-epoch argmax LightGBM posteriors, temporal smoothing continuity constraint. ’s mrpheus bug – ’s underlying YASA-parity classifier designed work, YASA doesn’t smooth default either. isolated single-epoch flips expected precisely model genuinely uncertain, sign something broken. ’s hypothesis, though, conclusion. staging carries posterior probabilities, ’s checkable directly: scattered REM epochs concentrated among low-confidence calls? Isolated single-epoch REM calls carry meaningfully lower confidence REM epochs part sustained, multi-minute run. ’s consistent “genuine borderline calls,” processing error – N1/REM well-known confusion pair automatic staging, since look comparatively wake-like EEG mainly told apart EMG atonia EOG, noisier signals.
code: stage <- stage_labels[apply(probs, 1, which.max)] rl <- rle(staging$stage) staging$run_id     <- rep(seq_along(rl$lengths), rl$lengths) run_lengths         <- aggregate(epoch ~ run_id, staging, length) staging$run_length <- run_lengths$epoch[match(staging$run_id, run_lengths$run_id)] staging$confidence <- pmax(staging$prob_W, staging$prob_N1, staging$prob_N2,                            staging$prob_N3, staging$prob_REM)  isolated_rem  <- staging$confidence[staging$stage == "REM" & staging$run_length == 1] sustained_rem <- staging$confidence[staging$stage == "REM" & staging$run_length >= 5]  summary(isolated_rem) #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>  0.2751  0.3972  0.4441  0.4632  0.5059  0.8922 summary(sustained_rem) #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>  0.3295  0.4481  0.5408  0.5720  0.6814  0.8640

title: Cleaning up and interpreting real staging: a worked example with mrpheus
text: Confirmed--expected doesn’t mean desirable downstream analysis, though. single-epoch REM blip otherwise clear NREM sleep usually implausible even classifier briefly favoured , ’s worth cleaning cycle detection sees . Worth checking ’s actually left rather assuming smoothing solved everything: remaining REM runs 3.5-8.5 minutes long – individually plausible REM bout durations, noise. simply separate REM excursions across night one ultradian cycle count predict. Pushing min_run_epochs higher mean deleting real multi-minute REM bouts force count , wrong lever pull. compute_cycles(method = "aasm")’s gap-tolerance exists exactly situation – fragmented REM separated brief interruptions shouldn’t count separate cycles: Still high. remaining explanation: REM excursions sitting daytime Wake, far main nocturnal cluster, getting counted “cycle” simply nothing else nearby.
code: hyp_smooth <- smooth_hypnogram(hyp, method = c("aasm_isolated", "min_run"), min_run_epochs = 4) mean(hyp_smooth$stage != hyp_smooth$stage_raw) #> [1] 0.1283019 rl_smooth <- rle(as.character(hyp_smooth$stage)) table(rl_smooth$lengths[rl_smooth$values == "REM"]) #>  #>  1  2  3  4  6  7  9 10 12 13 14 17  #>  1  2  1  3  1  6  1  1  1  1  2  2 nrow(compute_cycles(hyp_smooth, method = "aasm")) #> [1] 10

what: Windowing: isolating the real sleep period
title: Cleaning up and interpreting real staging: a worked example with mrpheus
text: SC4001 22-hour ambulatory recording – ordinary daytime Wake one real sleep opportunity. window_hypnogram() restricts analysis just sleep period. data-driven version (first sleep epoch last sleep epoch) reliable borrowing illustrative epoch range plotting example, since plot-oriented slice isn’t guaranteed land exactly true sleep offset: duration expected 7-8 hour range, cycle count now sits physiologically ordinary range – confirming earlier inflated count really daytime-Wake artifact analysing whole recording, something smoothing fixed.
code: sleep_idx    <- which(as.character(hyp_smooth$stage) != "W") onset_epoch  <- hyp_smooth$epoch[sleep_idx[1]] offset_epoch <- hyp_smooth$epoch[sleep_idx[length(sleep_idx)]] (offset_epoch - onset_epoch + 1) * 30 / 3600  # duration in hours -- sanity check #> [1] 15.48333  hyp_sleep <- window_hypnogram(hyp_smooth, from_epoch = onset_epoch, to_epoch = offset_epoch) nrow(compute_cycles(hyp_sleep, method = "aasm")) #> [1] 10

title: Cleaning up and interpreting real staging: a worked example with mrpheus
text: properly windowed hypnogram, ’s worth seeing much "aasm"’s gap-tolerance actually changes versus strict "feinberg_floyd" method: feinberg_floyd (interruption tolerance) tends fragment aggressively aasm (tolerates brief non-REM interruptions rem_gap_min minutes) – method “right” depends whether brief arousal mid-REM end REM period , genuinely debatable scoring choice rather settled fact.
code: cyc_ff   <- compute_cycles(hyp_sleep, method = "feinberg_floyd") cyc_aasm <- compute_cycles(hyp_sleep, method = "aasm")  nrow(cyc_ff) #> [1] 15 nrow(cyc_aasm) #> [1] 10

what: Both plotting styles, side by side
title: Cleaning up and interpreting real staging: a worked example with mrpheus
code: plot_hypnogram(hyp_sleep, cycles = cyc_aasm) plot_hypnogram(hyp_sleep, style = "capsule")

title: Cleaning up and interpreting real staging: a worked example with mrpheus
text: Scattered, brief stage flips raw automatic staging expected behaviour unsmoothed per-epoch classifier, automatically bug – check confidence/run-length assuming otherwise. Smoothing (smooth_hypnogram()) cleans genuine single-epoch noise, isn’t substitute windowing, pushing far starts deleting real short sleep events rather noise. Sleep architecture cycle analyses almost always windowed actual sleep period (window_hypnogram()) interpretation, especially ambulatory naturalistic recordings include substantial pre-/post-sleep Wake. compute_cycles()’s two methods encode real, debatable scoring decision (interruption tolerance), just implementation detail – worth checking rather trusting one blindly. See zeitR-integration kind worked example coarse, actigraphy-derived side – different staging source, different caveats (-wrist time, multi-night windowing), habit checking rather assuming.

title: Coarse hypnograms from actigraphy: a worked example with zeitR
text: zeitR ships real validation recording, input1.txt, used pipeline-parity tests Python reference implementation: quiet = TRUE deliberate, just noise-suppression – worth checking ’s actually quieting rather reflexively silencing : handful minor timestamp gaps, backward jumps, year artefacts. Small gaps like common real wearable recordings (missed transmission, brief disconnect) don’t necessarily invalidate recording – ’s worth actually looking $issues rather assuming quiet = TRUE means “nothing see .”
code: result <- zeitR::run_pipeline(   system.file("extdata", "input1.txt", package = "zeitR"),   tz    = "UTC",   quiet = TRUE ) result result$issues #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>     row datetime            issue detail                       #>   <int> <dttm>              <chr> <chr>                        #> 1 10146 2021-06-03 12:50:54 gap   2199 s gap before this epoch #> 2 20141 2021-06-10 11:43:44 gap   1130 s gap before this epoch #> 3 30237 2021-06-17 12:01:57 gap   193 s gap before this epoch  #> 4 49255 2021-06-30 17:19:12 gap   1215 s gap before this epoch #> 5 60894 2021-07-08 19:54:25 gap   2233 s gap before this epoch

title: Coarse hypnograms from actigraphy: a worked example with zeitR
text: Coarse resolution, auto-detected correctly (N1/N2/N3/REM labels data). nrow(hyp) likely well beyond one night’s worth epochs – input1.txt multi-day recording, matters everything follows.
code: zeitr_hyp  <- zeitR::export_hypnogram(result) hyp        <- new_hypnogram(zeitr_hyp) attr(hyp, "resolution") #> [1] "coarse" nrow(hyp) #> [1] 76196

what: A caveat: off-wrist time is folded into “Wake”
title: Coarse hypnograms from actigraphy: a worked example with zeitR
text: zeitR::export_hypnogram()’s stage mapping sends state == 4 (-wrist) "W", label genuine wakefulness – ’s fourth “unknown/-wrist” category hypnoR’s 3-state coarse model. ’s reasonable simplification, bug, means recording substantial -wrist time show inflated Wake duration isn’t really sleep/wake behaviour . Worth checking much recording actually : proportion small, ’s non-issue . given analysis turned unexpectedly long Wake/WASO specific night, first place check concluding anything sleep quality – device simply wrist.
code: offwrist_epochs <- sum(result$data$state == 4L) offwrist_epochs #> [1] 5542 offwrist_epochs / nrow(result$data)  # proportion of the whole recording #> [1] 0.07273348

title: Coarse hypnograms from actigraphy: a worked example with zeitR
text: input1.txt spans several nights (result$nights), running metrics entire multi-day recording exactly kind mistake covered mrpheus article: TST represent multiple nights’ sleep combined, SOL measured start whole file rather single night’s actual bedtime. Restrict one real night using bed_time/get_up_time – exactly lights_off/lights_on window compute_sleep_architecture() window_hypnogram() expect:

what: Does smoothing matter here the way it did for mrpheus?
title: Coarse hypnograms from actigraphy: a worked example with zeitR
text: mrpheus article found smoothing genuinely necessary, mrpheus::stage_epochs() raw per-epoch argmax classifier continuity constraint. zeitR’s pipeline different: Crespo (2012)’s sleep-period detection Cole-Kripke (1992)’s epoch scoring impose temporal structure output, rather staging epoch independently. Worth checking whether smooth_hypnogram() actually finds anything , rather assuming lesson transfers automatically: Whatever number comes , compare mrpheus article’s equivalent check – ’s much lower , ’s evidence two staging sources genuinely differ much raw epoch-level noise produce, just coincidence particular recording.
code: hyp_smooth <- smooth_hypnogram(hyp_night1, method = c("aasm_isolated", "min_run"), min_run_epochs = 4) mean(hyp_smooth$stage != hyp_smooth$stage_raw) #> [1] 0.07159353

title: Coarse hypnograms from actigraphy: a worked example with zeitR
text: compute_cycles() remains AASM-regardless staging source – coarse hypnograms REM stage segment NREM/REM cycles :
code: arch_night1 <- compute_sleep_architecture(hyp_night1) arch_night1 #> # A tibble: 1 × 14 #>   tst_min tib_min se_pct sol_min waso_min rem_lat_min sws_lat_min pct_n1 pct_n2 #>     <dbl>   <dbl>  <dbl>   <dbl>    <dbl>       <dbl>       <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1     207    216.   95.6       0        9          NA          NA     NA     NA #> # ℹ 5 more variables: pct_n3 <dbl>, pct_rem <dbl>, pct_sleep <dbl>, #> #   pct_quiet_sleep <dbl>, staging_resolution <chr> trans_night1 <- compute_transitions(hyp_night1) trans_night1$matrix #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>   from        `Quiet sleep`  Sleep       W #>   <chr>               <dbl>  <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1 Quiet sleep         0.953 0.0413 0.00517 #> 2 Sleep               0.519 0.111  0.370   #> 3 W                   0.222 0.389  0.389 trans_night1$fragmentation #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   n_transitions fragmentation_index wake_transitions #>           <int>               <dbl>            <int> #> 1            53               0.123               12 compute_cycles(hyp_night1) #> Error in `compute_cycles()`: #> ! `compute_cycles()` requires a full AASM hypnogram. #> ✖ `hypnogram` has "coarse" resolution (no REM stage), so NREM/REM cycles cannot #>   be detected.

title: Coarse hypnograms from actigraphy: a worked example with zeitR
code: plot_hypnogram(hyp_night1) plot_hypnogram(hyp_night1, style = "capsule") plot_architecture(arch_night1)

what: Main sleep vs naps, if there are any
title: Coarse hypnograms from actigraphy: a worked example with zeitR
text: result$nights$is_nap distinguishes zeitR’s main sleep periods secondary (nap) periods. Worth checking whether recording naps assuming single “sleep period” exists per day: naps present, comparing architecture main night (rather pooling together) avoids quietly averaging two physiologically different kinds sleep opportunity one number.
code: table(result$nights$is_nap) #>  #> FALSE  #>    55

title: Coarse hypnograms from actigraphy: a worked example with zeitR
text: Actigraphy-derived hypnograms carry different caveats automatic PSG staging – -wrist time masquerading Wake zeitR-specific one check, way scattered low-confidence REM mrpheus-specific one. quiet = TRUE (warning-suppression argument) worth checking ’s actually suppressing, just trusting silently. Windowing real analysis period matters regardless staging source – multi-night actigraphy recording needs care multi-hour ambulatory PSG recording . Smoothing’s necessity property staging source, universal step always apply – checking whether changes anything honest assuming lesson one pipeline transfers unchanged another. compute_cycles()’s AASM-requirement genuine constraint coarse staging can support, arbitrary limitation – ’s REM stage 3-state model segment cycles .

what: hypnoR — Hypnogram handling and sleep architecture metrics for R
title: hypnoR — Hypnogram handling and sleep architecture metrics for R
text: Hypnogram handling, plotting, sleep architecture metrics R — staging-agnostic layer Circadia Lab ecosystem.  ⚠️ hypnoR early development formally tested. API may change without notice, estimation results yet validated reference implementation, package undergone peer review. Use caution verify outputs independently using research context.

title: hypnoR — Hypnogram handling and sleep architecture metrics for R
text: hypnoR hypnogram layer Circadia Lab R ecosystem. sits raw signal sources integrated participant database, providing common interface sleep staging regardless staging came . accepts two staging resolutions: Full AASM (5-state: W / N1 / N2 / N3 / REM) — supplied mrpheus Coarse (3-state: W / Sleep / Quiet sleep) — supplied zeitR metric plotting functions resolution-agnostic: compute every metric possible given available stages return NA (documented per-function) metrics need finer staging ’s available. Downstream, hypnoR feeds syncR::sync() part unified participant-indexed database.

title: hypnoR — Hypnogram handling and sleep architecture metrics for R
text: 🏗️ new_hypnogram() — constructor everything else goes . Accepts bare tibble epoch/stage columns (e.g. zeitR::export_hypnogram() output) mrpheus_hypnogram object (mrpheus::export_hypnogram() output), auto-detecting AASM vs coarse resolution normalising one representation. 🧹 smooth_hypnogram() — cleans isolated single-epoch stage flips raw, unsmoothed per-epoch classifiers, via two label-rules (aasm_isolated, min_run), applicable independently sequence. 🪟 window_hypnogram() — restricts hypnogram time (lights_off/lights_on) epoch (from_epoch/to_epoch) window metric sees . 📐 compute_sleep_architecture() — TST, TIB, SE, SOL, WASO, REM/SWS latency, stage percentages; degrades gracefully coarse hypnograms. 🌙 compute_cycles() — NREM/REM cycle segmentation via Feinberg & Floyd (1979) (method = "feinberg_floyd") gap-tolerant variant (method = "aasm"). Full AASM staging . 🔀 compute_transitions() — stage--stage transition matrix (counts probabilities) plus fragmentation index wake-transition count. 🎨 plot_hypnogram(), plot_architecture(), plot_transition_matrix() — publication-ready plots built ggplot2 (runtime-checked, hard, dependency) shared Circadia Lab colour palette. plot_hypnogram() supports two visual styles: "step" (classic clinical trace) "capsule" (rounded-pill bars per stage run, one lane per stage).

title: hypnoR — Hypnogram handling and sleep architecture metrics for R
text: read_hypnogram() — direct ingestion CSV, EDF annotations, YASA output, Compumedics Profusion, Nox Medical files. yet implemented; now, hypnograms come upstream package (mrpheus::export_hypnogram(), zeitR::export_hypnogram()) hand-built tibble passed new_hypnogram().

title: hypnoR — Hypnogram handling and sleep architecture metrics for R
code: hypnoR/ ├── R/ │   ├── hypnoR-package.R        # package-level docs │   ├── new_hypnogram.R         # new_hypnogram() constructor │   ├── smooth.R                # smooth_hypnogram() │   ├── window_hypnogram.R      # window_hypnogram() │   ├── architecture.R          # compute_sleep_architecture() │   ├── cycles.R                # compute_cycles() │   ├── transitions.R           # compute_transitions() │   ├── read_hypnogram.R        # ingestion (not yet implemented) │   ├── plot.R                  # plot_hypnogram(), plot_architecture(), │   │                           #   plot_transition_matrix() │   └── utils.R                 # internal helpers, stage colour palette ├── tests/testthat/ ├── vignettes/ │   ├── getting-started.Rmd │   └── articles/ │       ├── mrpheus-integration.Rmd  # worked example: AASM/mrpheus │       └── zeitR-integration.Rmd    # worked example: coarse/zeitR ├── man/figures/                # logo, favicon, card ├── .github/workflows/          # R CMD CHECK + pkgdown/coverage CI ├── _pkgdown.yml └── DESCRIPTION

title: hypnoR — Hypnogram handling and sleep architecture metrics for R
text: Prerequisites: R ≥ 4.1. Install r-universe (recommended — pre-built binaries): install development version GitHub: reproduce vignettes real evaluated output (rather just code), also install mrpheus zeitR r-universe: Basic workflow (see vignette("getting-started") full walkthrough, including staging/result come case):
code: install.packages(   "hypnoR",   repos = c("https://circadia-bio.r-universe.dev", "https://cloud.r-project.org") ) # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("circadia-bio/hypnoR") install.packages(   c("mrpheus", "zeitR"),   repos = c("https://circadia-bio.r-universe.dev", "https://cloud.r-project.org") ) library(hypnoR)  # From mrpheus (full AASM) hyp <- new_hypnogram(mrpheus::export_hypnogram(staging, epoch_s = 30))  # ...or from zeitR (coarse) -- same functions from here on, either way hyp <- new_hypnogram(zeitR::export_hypnogram(result))  hyp   <- smooth_hypnogram(hyp)          # optional cleanup arch  <- compute_sleep_architecture(hyp) trans <- compute_transitions(hyp) cyc   <- compute_cycles(hyp)            # full AASM only  plot_hypnogram(hyp, cycles = cyc) plot_architecture(arch) plot_transition_matrix(trans$matrix)

title: hypnoR — Hypnogram handling and sleep architecture metrics for R
text: ⌚️ zeitR — wrist actigraphy analysis circadian metrics; upstream source coarse hypnograms 🪉 mrpheus — PSG signal analysis; upstream source full AASM hypnograms 🔄 syncR — integrates zeitR, slumbR, tallieR, hypnoR unified participant-indexed database 🔬 circadia-bio — Circadia Lab GitHub organisation

title: hypnoR — Hypnogram handling and sleep architecture metrics for R
text: Released MIT License. Copyright © Lucas França, Mario Leocadio-Miguel, 2026

title: Detect NREM/REM sleep cycles — compute_cycles
text: Segments full AASM hypnogram NREM/REM cycles. cycle defined NREM stretch followed qualifying REM period; complete cycles returned – trailing NREM stretch end recording followed qualifying REM period produce final row.

title: Detect NREM/REM sleep cycles — compute_cycles
code: compute_cycles(   hypnogram,   method = c("feinberg_floyd", "aasm"),   min_rem_epochs = 5L,   rem_gap_min = 15 )

title: Detect NREM/REM sleep cycles — compute_cycles
text: hypnogram hypnor_hypnogram object returned new_hypnogram() read_hypnogram(), data frame minimum epoch stage columns – passed new_hypnogram() automatically already hypnor_hypnogram. Epoch duration read object's epoch_sec attribute. method "feinberg_floyd" (default) "aasm": "feinberg_floyd" Feinberg & Floyd (1979)'s original, simplest rule: REM period maximal contiguous run REM epochs least min_rem_epochs. tolerance interruption – single non-REM epoch ends REM period. "aasm" simplified AASM-compatible variant tolerates brief non-REM interruptions within REM period: consecutive REM runs separated gap rem_gap_min minutes merged single REM period min_rem_epochs threshold applied combined REM epoch count. practical approximation, citable AASM standard – AASM manual define sleep-cycle segmentation rules. min_rem_epochs Minimum number REM epochs (merging method = "aasm") run qualify REM period. Default 5 (2.5 min 30-s epochs). rem_gap_min used method = "aasm". Maximum gap, minutes, non-REM epochs two REM runs merged single REM period. Default 15.

title: Detect NREM/REM sleep cycles — compute_cycles
text: tibble one row per detected (complete) cycle: cycle Integer cycle index. start_epoch,end_epoch First last epoch cycle, using values hypnogram$epoch – row position. differ whenever hypnogram start epoch 1 (e.g. subsetting larger recording sleep-period window). nrem_min,rem_min Duration NREM REM portions cycle (minutes). nrem_min whole non-REM portion cycle, including brief interruption epochs absorbed merged REM period method = "aasm"; rem_min counts actual REM epochs. nrem_min + rem_min == cycle_min. cycle_min Total cycle duration (minutes). Zero rows qualifying REM period found (e.g. sleep, REM ).

title: Detect NREM/REM sleep cycles — compute_cycles
text: applicable full AASM hypnograms (coarse actigraphy-derived hypnograms REM stage cycle-segmented); function errors coarse hypnogram rather silently returning empty result.

title: Detect NREM/REM sleep cycles — compute_cycles
text: Feinberg, ., & Floyd, T. C. (1979). Systematic trends across night human sleep cycles. Psychophysiology, 16(3), 283-291.

title: Detect NREM/REM sleep cycles — compute_cycles
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ hyp <- read_hypnogram("night_001.csv") compute_cycles(hyp) compute_cycles(hyp, method = "aasm", rem_gap_min = 10) } # }

title: Compute sleep architecture metrics — compute_sleep_architecture
text: Derives standard sleep architecture summary statistics staged hypnogram. metrics resolution-agnostic: function computes every metric possible given available staging levels returns NA metrics require stages present detected resolution (e.g. REM/SWS latency AASM stage percentages require full AASM hypnogram).

title: Compute sleep architecture metrics — compute_sleep_architecture
code: compute_sleep_architecture(hypnogram, lights_off = NULL, lights_on = NULL)

title: Compute sleep architecture metrics — compute_sleep_architecture
text: hypnogram hypnor_hypnogram object returned new_hypnogram() read_hypnogram(), data frame minimum epoch stage columns – passed new_hypnogram() automatically already hypnor_hypnogram. Epoch duration staging resolution read object's epoch_sec resolution attributes. lights_off, lights_on Optional POSIXct timestamps lights-lights-. supplied, hypnogram first restricted window via window_hypnogram() – every metric (TST, SOL, WASO, stage percentages, everything) computed relative window, just TIB/SE. Otherwise TIB defaults full span hypnogram passed (first last epoch).

title: Compute sleep architecture metrics — compute_sleep_architecture
text: one-row tibble columns: tst_min Total sleep time (minutes). tib_min Time bed (minutes). se_pct Sleep efficiency (percent) = TST / TIB * 100. sol_min Sleep onset latency (minutes). NA sleep epoch present. waso_min Wake sleep onset (minutes): wake epochs first last sleep epoch. NA sleep epoch present. rem_lat_min REM latency sleep onset (minutes). NA coarse hypnograms REM epoch present. sws_lat_min SWS (N3) latency sleep onset (minutes). NA coarse hypnograms N3 epoch present. pct_n1,pct_n2,pct_n3,pct_rem Stage percentages TST. NA coarse hypnograms. pct_sleep,pct_quiet_sleep Stage percentages TST coarse hypnograms. NA full AASM hypnograms. staging_resolution "aasm" "coarse".

title: Compute sleep architecture metrics — compute_sleep_architecture
text: Sleep onset defined first epoch non-"W" stage – applies uniformly full AASM hypnograms (N1 counts sleep onset) coarse hypnograms ("Sleep" "Quiet sleep" count).

title: Compute sleep architecture metrics — compute_sleep_architecture
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ hyp <- read_hypnogram("night_001.csv") compute_sleep_architecture(hyp) } # }

title: Compute stage-transition statistics — compute_transitions
text: Builds stage--stage transition probability matrix derives fragmentation indices staged hypnogram. Works full AASM coarse actigraphy-derived staging.

title: Compute stage-transition statistics — compute_transitions
code: compute_transitions(hypnogram, normalise = TRUE, include_wake = TRUE)

title: Compute stage-transition statistics — compute_transitions
text: hypnogram hypnor_hypnogram object returned new_hypnogram() read_hypnogram(), data frame minimum epoch stage columns – passed new_hypnogram() automatically already hypnor_hypnogram. normalise TRUE (default), row transition count matrix divided row sum give transition probabilities. Rows -stage never visited returned NA rather NaN. FALSE, raw transition counts returned. include_wake TRUE (default), "W" included state transition matrix like stage (including W->W self-transitions). FALSE, matrix restricted sleep-stage transitions : transition "W" excluded matrix built.

title: Compute stage-transition statistics — compute_transitions
text: list two elements: matrix tibble one row per stage: column plus one numeric column per stage (transition probabilities counts). fragmentation one-row tibble : n_transitions Number epoch--epoch stage changes (self-transitions count). fragmentation_index Proportion epochs followed different stage. wake_transitions Number transitions Wake (proxy arousal burden).

title: Compute stage-transition statistics — compute_transitions
text: Fragmentation metrics (n_transitions, fragmentation_index, wake_transitions) always computed full epoch sequence, wake included, regardless include_wake – argument controls shape returned matrix.

title: Compute stage-transition statistics — compute_transitions
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ hyp  <- read_hypnogram("night_001.csv") trans <- compute_transitions(hyp) trans$matrix trans$fragmentation  # Sleep-stage transitions only, excluding Wake compute_transitions(hyp, include_wake = FALSE) } # }

what: hypnoR: Hypnogram Handling, Plotting, and Sleep Architecture Metrics — hypnoR-package
title: hypnoR: Hypnogram Handling, Plotting, and Sleep Architecture Metrics — hypnoR-package
text: Provides staging-agnostic layer hypnogram ingestion, sleep architecture metric computation, cycle segmentation, transition analysis. Accepts full AASM-staged hypnograms (W / N1 / N2 / N3 / REM) mrpheus coarser actigraphy-derived hypnograms (W / Sleep / Quiet sleep) zeitR; metric functions degrade gracefully depending staging resolution. Includes publication-ready plotting via theme_circadia(). Designed hypnogram layer Circadia Lab ecosystem, feeding syncR::sync().

title: hypnoR: Hypnogram Handling, Plotting, and Sleep Architecture Metrics — hypnoR-package
text: Maintainer: Lucas França lucas.franca@northumbria.ac.uk (ORCID) Authors: Lucas França lucas.franca@northumbria.ac.uk (ORCID) Mario Leocadio-Miguel mario.miguel@northumbria.ac.uk (ORCID)

what: Construct a hypnoR hypnogram object — new_hypnogram
title: Construct a hypnoR hypnogram object — new_hypnogram
text: new_hypnogram() low-level constructor hypnor_hypnogram class used throughout hypnoR. normalises hypnogram-shaped input – whether bare tibble (produced zeitR::export_hypnogram() read_hypnogram()) specially classed object (currently: mrpheus::export_hypnogram()'s mrpheus_hypnogram) – single internal representation every hypnoR function expects.

title: Construct a hypnoR hypnogram object — new_hypnogram
code: new_hypnogram(x, ...)  # Default S3 method new_hypnogram(   x,   epoch_sec = NULL,   resolution = NULL,   subject_id = NULL,   source = NULL,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'mrpheus_hypnogram' new_hypnogram(   x,   epoch_sec = NULL,   resolution = NULL,   subject_id = NULL,   source = NULL,   ... )

title: Construct a hypnoR hypnogram object — new_hypnogram
text: x data frame minimum epoch stage columns, object dedicated new_hypnogram() method (currently: mrpheus_hypnogram). Recognised optional columns: time (POSIXct), subject_id, source. ... Passed methods; currently unused. epoch_sec Epoch duration seconds. x carries epoch duration (e.g. mrpheus_hypnogram's epoch_s attribute) value used unless epoch_sec explicitly supplied . Falls back 30L value can determined either source. resolution "aasm", "coarse", NULL (default). NULL, resolution auto-detected stage labels present x (, mrpheus_hypnogram input, taken resolution attribute). subject_id Character NULL. Overrides subject/participant identifier carried x. source Character NULL. Overrides source/scorer label carried x.

title: Construct a hypnoR hypnogram object — new_hypnogram
text: tibble class hypnor_hypnogram columns epoch (integer), time (POSIXct, NA unknown), stage (ordered factor), subject_id (character, NA unknown), source (character, NA unknown). detected epoch_sec resolution attached attributes.

title: Construct a hypnoR hypnogram object — new_hypnogram
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # From a bare tibble (e.g. zeitR::export_hypnogram() output) new_hypnogram(zeitr_hyp)  # From mrpheus::export_hypnogram() output new_hypnogram(mrpheus_hyp) } # }

what: Plot sleep architecture as a bar chart — plot_architecture
title: Plot sleep architecture as a bar chart — plot_architecture
text: Renders stage durations percentages horizontal bar chart using ggplot2 Circadia Lab colour palette.

title: Plot sleep architecture as a bar chart — plot_architecture
code: plot_architecture(   architecture,   metric = c("duration", "percentage"),   colours = NULL,   title = NULL )

title: Plot sleep architecture as a bar chart — plot_architecture
text: architecture tibble returned compute_sleep_architecture() – either single row, multiple rows comparing several nights, case night id column (present) used facet plot one panel per night. metric "duration" (minutes, default) "percentage" TST. colours Named character vector stage colours. See plot_hypnogram() defaults. title Optional plot title.

title: Plot sleep architecture as a bar chart — plot_architecture
text: ggplot object.

title: Plot sleep architecture as a bar chart — plot_architecture
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ hyp  <- read_hypnogram("night_001.csv") arch <- compute_sleep_architecture(hyp) plot_architecture(arch) plot_architecture(arch, metric = "percentage") } # }

title: Plot a hypnogram — plot_hypnogram
text: Renders hypnogram using ggplot2 Circadia Lab colour palette. Accepts full AASM coarse staging; lane order (deepest sleep bottom, Wake top) colour mapping set automatically staging levels present hypnogram.

title: Plot a hypnogram — plot_hypnogram
code: plot_hypnogram(   hypnogram,   style = c("step", "capsule"),   x_axis = c("auto", "time", "hours"),   date_breaks = "2 hours",   cycles = NULL,   colours = NULL,   title = NULL,   corner_min = 9 )

title: Plot a hypnogram — plot_hypnogram
text: hypnogram hypnor_hypnogram object returned new_hypnogram() read_hypnogram(), data frame minimum epoch stage columns – passed new_hypnogram() automatically already hypnor_hypnogram. Epoch duration read object's epoch_sec attribute. style "step" (default) "capsule": "step" Classic clinical step-plot: one line tracing stage every epoch. "capsule" Rounded-pill bars per contiguous stage run, one lane per stage. x_axis "auto" (default), "time", "hours": "auto" Uses actual clock time (time column) hypnogram carries non-NA timestamps, otherwise falls back elapsed hours since first epoch. "time" Forces clock time; errors time entirely NA (start_time supplied new_hypnogram() mrpheus::export_hypnogram()). "hours" Forces elapsed hours since first epoch, regardless whether real timestamps available. date_breaks used plotting clock time. Passed ggplot2::scale_x_datetime()'s date_breaks. Default "2 hours". cycles Optional: tibble returned compute_cycles(). supplied, dashed vertical line drawn start cycle. colours Named character vector mapping stage labels hex colours. Defaults built-palette drawn Circadia Lab colours. Pass named vector override. title Optional plot title. corner_min used style = "capsule". Maximum pill corner radius, minutes. Runs shorter 2 * corner_min get proportionally smaller radius (brief runs render fully rounded capsule/stadium shapes rather oversized corners), longer runs capped radius. Default 9.

title: Plot a hypnogram — plot_hypnogram
text: ggplot object.

title: Plot a hypnogram — plot_hypnogram
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ hyp <- read_hypnogram("night_001.csv") plot_hypnogram(hyp) plot_hypnogram(hyp, cycles = compute_cycles(hyp)) plot_hypnogram(hyp, x_axis = "hours") plot_hypnogram(hyp, style = "capsule") } # }

title: Plot a stage-transition heatmap — plot_transition_matrix
text: Renders transition probability (count) matrix returned compute_transitions() heatmap using ggplot2.

title: Plot a stage-transition heatmap — plot_transition_matrix
code: plot_transition_matrix(   transitions,   label_values = TRUE,   digits = 2L,   title = NULL )

title: Plot a stage-transition heatmap — plot_transition_matrix
text: transitions matrix element list returned compute_transitions(): tibble column plus one numeric column per stage. label_values TRUE (default), cell values printed inside tile. NA cells (unvisited -stages) left blank. digits Number decimal places cell labels. Default 2. title Optional plot title.

title: Plot a stage-transition heatmap — plot_transition_matrix
text: ggplot object.

title: Plot a stage-transition heatmap — plot_transition_matrix
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ hyp   <- read_hypnogram("night_001.csv") trans <- compute_transitions(hyp) plot_transition_matrix(trans$matrix) } # }

what: Read a hypnogram from file — read_hypnogram
title: Read a hypnogram from file — read_hypnogram
text: Imports staged hypnogram common formats returns tidy tibble one row per epoch.  resulting object standard input hypnoR functions.

title: Read a hypnogram from file — read_hypnogram
code: read_hypnogram(   path,   format = "auto",   epoch_sec = 30L,   tz = "UTC",   quiet = FALSE )

title: Read a hypnogram from file — read_hypnogram
text: path Path hypnogram file. format One "csv", "edf_annotations", "yasa", "compumedics", "nox", "auto" (default).  "auto", format inferred file extension header. epoch_sec Epoch duration seconds.  Default 30. tz Time zone string passed lubridate::as_datetime(). Default "UTC". quiet Suppress informational messages.  Default FALSE.

title: Read a hypnogram from file — read_hypnogram
text: tibble columns: epoch Integer epoch index (1-based). time POSIXct timestamp start epoch. stage Ordered factor.  Level set depends staging resolution: full AASM (W, N1, N2, N3, REM) coarse actigraphy-derived (W, Sleep, Quiet sleep). source Character — originating device / scorer label, available file header.

title: Read a hypnogram from file — read_hypnogram
text: hypnoR distinguishes two staging resolutions: Full AASM (5-state): supplied mrpheus. Coarse (3-state): supplied zeitR. downstream metric functions resolution-agnostic: compute possible given available stages document metrics require full AASM staging.

title: Read a hypnogram from file — read_hypnogram
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ hyp <- read_hypnogram("night_001.csv") hyp <- read_hypnogram("night_001.edf", format = "edf_annotations") } # }

what: Smooth a hypnogram by reassigning short, likely-spurious runs — smooth_hypnogram
title: Smooth a hypnogram by reassigning short, likely-spurious runs — smooth_hypnogram
text: Raw automatic sleep staging typically per-epoch classification temporal continuity constraint (e.g. mrpheus::stage_epochs() pure per-epoch argmax posterior probabilities), can produce brief, isolated stage flips inconsistent sleep physiology – single REM epoch nested inside N2 run, instance. smooth_hypnogram() offers two independent, rule-based cleanup passes operate purely stage labels (posterior probabilities required, none carried new_hypnogram() case).

title: Smooth a hypnogram by reassigning short, likely-spurious runs — smooth_hypnogram
code: smooth_hypnogram(hypnogram, method = "aasm_isolated", min_run_epochs = 2L)

title: Smooth a hypnogram by reassigning short, likely-spurious runs — smooth_hypnogram
text: hypnogram hypnor_hypnogram object returned new_hypnogram() read_hypnogram(), data frame minimum epoch stage columns – passed new_hypnogram() automatically already hypnor_hypnogram. method One "aasm_isolated" (default) "min_run". requested, "aasm_isolated" always applied first, "min_run" – regardless order two given method: "aasm_isolated" single epoch whose stage differs immediate neighbours reassigned neighbours' stage, neighbours share stage. mirrors conservative manual-scoring convention resolving ambiguous single epoch bounded identically sides. never touches run 2+ epochs, never touches isolated epoch whose two neighbours disagree . "min_run" run shorter min_run_epochs reassigned whichever flanking run longer (ties favour preceding run). Broader "aasm_isolated": require two flanking runs agree , (min_run_epochs > 2) can merge runs longer single epoch. Applied single left--right pass run-length structure computed start step – iterated fixed point, pathological hypnogram may still contain runs shorter min_run_epochs afterwards. Call smooth_hypnogram() result second pass needed. Note "aasm_isolated" ran first, may already merged runs together, changes flanking-run lengths "min_run" sees – two methods genuinely sequential, independent. min_run_epochs used "min_run". Minimum run length (epochs) leave untouched. Default 2L (merges single-epoch runs).

title: Smooth a hypnogram by reassigning short, likely-spurious runs — smooth_hypnogram
text: input hypnor_hypnogram, two changes: stage now holds smoothed labels, new stage_raw column preserves original, unsmoothed labels comparison/audit. columns attributes (epoch_sec, resolution) unchanged.

title: Smooth a hypnogram by reassigning short, likely-spurious runs — smooth_hypnogram
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ hyp        <- new_hypnogram(mrpheus_hyp) hyp_smooth <- smooth_hypnogram(hyp) mean(hyp_smooth$stage != hyp_smooth$stage_raw)  # proportion of epochs changed  # Broader cleanup: also merge non-isolated short runs smooth_hypnogram(hyp, method = c("aasm_isolated", "min_run"), min_run_epochs = 3) } # }

what: Restrict a hypnogram to a time or epoch window — window_hypnogram
title: Restrict a hypnogram to a time or epoch window — window_hypnogram
text: Filters hypnogram sub-range, either clock time (lights_off/lights_on) epoch number (from_epoch/to_epoch), re-attaching epoch_sec resolution correctly result. single place windowing logic lives hypnoR – compute_cycles() compute_transitions() lights_off/lights_on arguments ; window first, pass windowed hypnogram .

title: Restrict a hypnogram to a time or epoch window — window_hypnogram
code: window_hypnogram(   hypnogram,   lights_off = NULL,   lights_on = NULL,   from_epoch = NULL,   to_epoch = NULL )

title: Restrict a hypnogram to a time or epoch window — window_hypnogram
text: hypnogram hypnor_hypnogram object returned new_hypnogram() read_hypnogram(), data frame minimum epoch stage columns – passed new_hypnogram() automatically already hypnor_hypnogram. lights_off, lights_on POSIXct NULL. must supplied together. Requires hypnogram carry real timestamps (.e. start_time supplied new_hypnogram() mrpheus::export_hypnogram()) – errors time entirely NA. Mutually exclusive from_epoch/to_epoch. from_epoch, to_epoch Integer NULL. Either may supplied alone (open-ended window); defaults first/last epoch hypnogram respectively. Mutually exclusive lights_off/lights_on.

title: Restrict a hypnogram to a time or epoch window — window_hypnogram
text: hypnor_hypnogram, filtered window, epoch_sec resolution carried unchanged input – resolution re-detected (possibly much smaller) windowed subset, since short window plausibly contain REM/N3 epochs get misdetected "coarse" otherwise.

title: Restrict a hypnogram to a time or epoch window — window_hypnogram
text: compute_sleep_architecture()'s lights_off/lights_on arguments sugar calling first: passing restricts TST, SOL, WASO, every metric window, just TIB/SE denominator.

title: Restrict a hypnogram to a time or epoch window — window_hypnogram
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ hyp <- new_hypnogram(mrpheus_hyp)  # By clock time hyp_night <- window_hypnogram(   hyp,   lights_off = as.POSIXct("2024-01-01 23:00:00", tz = "UTC"),   lights_on  = as.POSIXct("2024-01-02 07:00:00", tz = "UTC") )  # By epoch range (e.g. isolating a sleep period out of a longer recording) hyp_sleep <- window_hypnogram(hyp, from_epoch = 1001L, to_epoch = 1950L)  compute_cycles(hyp_sleep) compute_transitions(hyp_sleep) } # }

title: hypnoR 0.1.1 (2026-07)
text: Added new_hypnogram(), constructor underlying hypnoR functions. Accepts either bare tibble epoch/stage columns (e.g. zeitR::export_hypnogram() output) mrpheus_hypnogram object (mrpheus::export_hypnogram() output), normalising single hypnor_hypnogram representation. Staging resolution (AASM vs coarse) auto-detected stage labels present. Implemented compute_sleep_architecture(): TST, TIB, SE, SOL, WASO, REM/SWS latency, stage percentages, resolution-agnostic. Sleep onset defined first non-"W" epoch AASM coarse hypnograms. epoch_sec staging resolution read automatically hypnor_hypnogram object rather passed arguments. Implemented compute_transitions(): stage--stage transition matrix (counts row-normalised probabilities) plus fragmentation index wake-transition count. New include_wake argument controls whether Wake included state matrix; fragmentation metrics always use full epoch sequence regardless setting. Implemented compute_cycles(): NREM/REM cycle segmentation full AASM hypnograms, two selectable algorithms. method = "feinberg_floyd" (default) treats maximal contiguous REM run least min_rem_epochs REM period, tolerance interruption. method = "aasm" merges REM runs separated gap rem_gap_min minutes (default 15) single REM period applying min_rem_epochs threshold. Errors coarse hypnograms, REM stage segment . Added smooth_hypnogram(), hypnogram-level cleanup step raw unsmoothed per-epoch staging (e.g. mrpheus::stage_epochs(), temporal continuity constraint can produce isolated single-epoch stage flips). Two label-rules, applicable independently sequence: "aasm_isolated" (default) reassigns single epoch flanked identically sides; "min_run" merges run shorter min_run_epochs whichever flanking run longer, regardless whether flanks agree. original labels preserved new stage_raw column. Added window_hypnogram(): restricts hypnogram time (lights_off/ lights_on) epoch (from_epoch/to_epoch) window, correctly preserving epoch_sec resolution result rather re-detecting (possibly much smaller) subset. single place windowing logic lives – compute_cycles() compute_transitions() lights_off/lights_on arguments ; window first, pass windowed hypnogram .

title: hypnoR 0.1.1 (2026-07)
text: Implemented plot_hypnogram(), plot_architecture(), plot_transition_matrix(). three use runtime ggplot2 check rather hard dependency (consistent rest ecosystem) share built-Circadia Lab stage colour palette. plot_hypnogram() takes optional cycles argument (tibble compute_cycles()) overlay cycle-boundary lines; plot_architecture() facets night/id column present, comparing nights. plot_hypnogram() gains x_axis argument ("auto" (default), "time", "hours"). hypnogram carries real timestamps (.e. start_time supplied new_hypnogram() mrpheus::export_hypnogram()), x-axis now shows actual clock time default instead always using elapsed hours since first epoch. plot_hypnogram() gains style argument: "step" (default, unchanged) "capsule" – rounded-pill bars per contiguous stage run, one lane per stage, loosely modelled Apple Watch sleep chart’s visual language (Circadia Lab palette, full AASM lane ordering, light theme rather Apple’s dark coarse 4-stage scheme). ggplot2 native rounded-rect geom, pills hand-traced polygons (corner radius scales bar’s width, via new corner_min argument). Rotated stage colour palette: N1 -> Wake -> REM -> N1. W now cream (N1’s), REM now amber (wake’s), N1 now teal (REM’s); N2/N3/Sleep/Quiet sleep unchanged.

title: hypnoR 0.1.1 (2026-07)
text: compute_cycles()’s start_epoch/end_epoch columns reporting row position within hypnogram, actual values hypnogram$epoch – identical whenever epoch runs 1:n (true every fixture test suite far), wrong hypnogram doesn’t start epoch 1, e.g. subsetting longer recording sleep-period window. Durations (nrem_min/rem_min/cycle_min) unaffected, since already computed row-position differences rather epoch column. Behaviour change: compute_sleep_architecture()’s lights_off/ lights_on arguments now restrict every metric via window_hypnogram(), just TIB/SE – previously TST, SOL, WASO, stage percentages computed entire hypnogram regardless window, inconsistent. Requires hypnogram carry real timestamps; errors time entirely NA.

title: hypnoR 0.1.1 (2026-07)
text: Fixed mrpheus/zeitR dependency resolution CI: added Additional_repositories: https://circadia-bio.r-universe.dev repo-root .Rprofile setting options(repos = ...) directly. Additional_repositories alone doesn’t automatically wire pak’s dependency resolution CI – ’s mainly documentation/NOTE- suppression field – .Rprofile actually makes packages resolvable. Added covr-based coverage step pkgdown.yaml workflow, writing coverage badge (docs/badges/coverage.json) gh-pages pushes main/master/release (matching zeitR’s setup). covr added Suggests; new dev/check_coverage.R running coverage locally. README gains R CMD CHECK Coverage badges.

title: hypnoR 0.1.1 (2026-07)
text: Rewrote vignette("getting-started") reflect actual current API (previous version referenced read_hypnogram(), doesn’t exist yet), run real recordings staging sources rather hypothetical placeholder code: mrpheus’s bundled SC4001E0 example AASM path, zeitR’s bundled input1.txt ActTrust recording (via full rest-activity pipeline) coarse path – demonstrating staging-agnostic contract concretely: compute_sleep_architecture() returns NA AASM-fields coarse hypnograms populates coarse-ones instead, compute_transitions() works 3x3 matrix instead 5x5, compute_cycles() errors clearly coarse staging (REM stage segment cycles ). mrpheus- zeitR-dependent chunks guarded requireNamespace(); packages added Suggests. New dev/test_mrpheus_pipeline.R dev/test_zeitR_pipeline.R. Added two worked-example articles (“Worked examples” pkgdown site), cross-linked : vignette("mrpheus-integration"), warts--walkthrough diagnosing scattered raw REM calls, smoothing, windowing real sleep period, comparing compute_cycles()’s two methods; vignette("zeitR-integration"), coarse-staging counterpart – timestamp-gap inspection, -wrist time folded "W", picking right night multi-day recording, checking (rather assuming) whether smoothing matters much actigraphy-derived staging mrpheus’s raw automatic staging.

title: hypnoR 0.1.0 (2026-06)
text: Package skeleton: read_hypnogram(), compute_sleep_architecture(), compute_cycles(), compute_transitions(), plot_hypnogram(), plot_architecture(), plot_transition_matrix(). Staging-agnostic design: metric functions accept full AASM (W / N1 / N2 / N3 / REM) coarse actigraphy-derived (W / Sleep / Quiet sleep) hypnograms. pkgdown site Bootstrap 5 Circadia Lab branding.
